title: Violation Letter Input
description: Validated input shape for renderViolationLetterBundlePdf. Letters are legally consequential — every fact in the rendered PDF must come from a field validated against this schema. Statutory wording is NOT in this schema; it's injected at render time from lib/enforcement/violation_letter_rules.js so we have ONE source of truth for Texas §209 language.

stage.description: The escalation stage. Determines title, tone, cure period, mailing class (first-class vs. certified), and whether hearing-rights block + SCRA disclosure render.

letter_date.description: ISO date the letter is being mailed (NOT the date it was drafted). Cure-by and hearing-by deadlines are computed from this date. Stamped at Mail Queue lock time.

street_address.description: PHYSICAL LOT address — never mailing. See CLAUDE.md SSOT table.

name.description: Owner of record, legal name as it appears on title.

mailing_address.description: Where the letter is mailed. NOT the property street address. For rentals, this is the off-site owner address.

honorific.description: Mr. / Mrs. / Mr. and Mrs. — used for salutation. Renderer falls back gracefully if absent.

legal_name.description: Full registered entity name, e.g., 'Waterview Estates Homeowners Association, Inc.' Used in formal salutations.

letter_cure_days_certified_209.description: Statute floor 30 days (Tex. Prop. Code §209.006). Default 30.

letter_pay_to_name.description: Payee on remittance instructions. Usually association legal_name.

logo_storage_path.description: Community logo for the co-branded header.

violations.description: One or more violation findings being bundled in this letter. Empty array is rejected — there's no such thing as a violation letter without a violation.

type.description: Short category — 'Lawn maintenance' / 'Trash bins visible' / 'Unapproved paint color' / etc.

description.description: Specific finding — what was observed, where, on what date. Not editorial language.

date_documented.description: Date the inspector documented the violation. Anchors the regulatory timeline.

prior_notice_dates.description: Dates of prior notices sent for this same violation. Required for courtesy_2 and certified_209 to satisfy §209's prior-notice expectation.

photo_url.description: Wide + close-up photos. Empty-chair lens: photos document for the homeowner's benefit, not adversarially.

governing_doc_reference.description: CC&R or rule citation — e.g., 'CC&R Article 5, Section 3' or 'Rules & Regs Section 12'.

fine_cents.description: Fine amount in cents. ONLY populated for stage = 'fine_assessed'. Defaults to community fine schedule if null.

wrong_house_verified.description: 5-signal wrong-house verification result (GPS + heading + polygon + AI house# + reviewer). MUST be true before any letter with this violation ships. See project_drv_module memory note.

certified_tracking_number.description: USPS certified mail tracking number. Required for certified_209 + fine_assessed stages when mail-queue locks.

include_scra_disclosure.description: Default true on certified_209 + fine_assessed. Servicemembers Civil Relief Act notice.

community_logo_buffer.description: PNG/JPEG Buffer of the community logo. Optional — renderer falls back to text-only header + Bedrock cornerstone if absent.

allOf.description: Fine stage requires at least one violation with fine_cents populated OR a community fine_schedule that the renderer can derive from
courtesy_2 + certified_209 should reference prior notice dates (not a hard reject, but logged as a warning)
